All reports conclude that this is benign, typical code, with no malicious behavior, obfuscation, or security risks.\n\n**Confirming the code's content:**\n\nThe original code snippet is:\n\n```js\n\"use strict\";\nObject.defineProperty(exports, \"__esModule\", { value: true });\n//# sourceMappingURL=config.model.js.map\n```\n\nThis matches the descriptions in all reports.\n\n---\n\n### Step 1: Are the reported issues present?\n\n- The reports correctly identify that the code is just a minimal module setup with a source map comment.\n- No data flows, no suspicious code, no malicious behavior.\n- The code does not perform any data processing, network activity, or file operations.\n\n**Conclusion:** No issues or malicious activity are present in the code.\n\n---\n\n### Step 2: Flaws or mistakes in the report's logic or reasoning\n\n- The reports are consistent and accurate in their assessment.\n- They correctly interpret the source map comment as a build artifact, not malicious.\n- No errors or flawed reasoning detected.\n\n**Note:** The reports are thorough and consistent.\n\n---\n\n### Step 3: Review the scores assigned\n\nScores are all:\n\n- confidence: 1\n- obfuscated: 0\n- malware: 0\n- securityRisk: 0\n\nThese seem appropriate given the code.\n\n**Suggestion:** No changes needed.
